template.name: PeriodicGenerator
template.declaration: // Releases a fresh job of periodic task 'id' every period, forever, // triggered by the scheduler's 'release' pulse when its timer is 0.

template.name: AperiodicGenerator
Scheduler
template.declaration: clock cpuClock; // measures exactly one time unit of CPU progress

query.comment: The network never deadlocks: the scheduler runs for unbounded time.

Schedulability over UNBOUNDED time: no periodic or aperiodic deadline is ever missed, for every admissible sporadic arrival pattern. This is the real TBS guarantee (U_p + U_s <= 1), not just evidence on one finite window.
The bounded job pool (MAX_APERIODIC) never overflows, which justifies the finite pool size for the rate-limited stream.
TBS bandwidth bound: the running server deadline stays within C_k/U_s of "now", i.e. aperiodic work is throttled to at most U_s.
Liveness: every aperiodic request placed in slot 0 is eventually completed (served, not starved).
